profiles.portfolio: Download sources suitable for a public portfolio demo. Includes non-commercial sources with clear labels.
profiles.commercial: Download only sources marked as commercial-safe by the manifest.
profiles.all: Download every enabled source in the manifest.

sources.name: MedlinePlus Health Topic XML
sources.license: Public domain health-topic summaries with MedlinePlus/NLM attribution requirements; avoid separately copyrighted A.D.A.M. and ASHP material.
sources.attribution: Source: MedlinePlus, National Library of Medicine.
sources.notes: Downloads the latest official health-topic XML, which contains many patient education topics.

sources.name: openFDA Drug Labeling Sample
sources.license: Generally public domain / CC0 unless endpoint documentation marks exceptions; no endorsement implied.
sources.attribution: Data provided by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (https://open.fda.gov).
sources.notes: Stores each label as a JSON document with indications, warnings, contraindications, dosage, adverse reactions, and interactions when present.

sources.name: PMC Open Access Commercial-Use Article Sample
sources.license: Article-level Creative Commons license required. Default filter keeps CC0, CC BY, CC BY-SA, and CC BY-ND and skips retracted records.
sources.attribution: PMC Open Access Subset, National Library of Medicine. Preserve article-level license and citation metadata.
sources.notes: Streams the official AWS OA commercial-use XML file list and downloads a small number of direct XML article files.

sources.name: CDC Public Health Pages
sources.license: Most CDC/ATSDR website information is public domain, with attribution, no endorsement, and third-party-content cautions.
sources.attribution: Source: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
sources.notes: Downloads a focused set of public health HTML pages for retrieval demos.

sources.name: WHO Open Access Guideline PDFs
sources.license: CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO for non-commercial use; preserve attribution and do not imply WHO endorsement.
sources.attribution: World Health Organization publications. Licence: CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO unless the publication states otherwise.
sources.notes: Good for a non-commercial portfolio demo; exclude from commercial freelance deployments unless permission/licensing is handled.

urls.title: WHO Guideline for the Pharmacological Treatment of Hypertension in Adults

sources.name: NICE Guidelines - Reference Only, Not Downloaded
sources.license: Current NICE reuse terms exclude AI use without permission.
sources.notes: Kept in the manifest to show license diligence and why this source is not part of the default RAG corpus.
